name: Early Islamic World
description: The Arabian Peninsula and the first centers of Islamic civilization.

name: Arabian Peninsula
description: The Arabian Peninsula - birthplace of Islam.

name: Persia / Iran
description: Persia and Greater Iran - heartland of Sufi literary culture.

name: Central Asia
description: Transoxiana and the Steppes - cradle of the Naqshbandiyya.

name: South Asia
description: The Indian Subcontinent - the largest Sufi sphere by population.

name: Ottoman World
description: The Ottoman Empire - where Sufi orders achieved state integration.

name: North Africa
description: The Maghreb and Egypt - centers of Shadhiliyya.

name: Al-Andalus
description: Islamic Iberia - the western pole of Islamic civilization.

name: Levant
description: Syria, Palestine, and Lebanon - Damascus a major center.

name: Sub-Saharan Africa
description: West and East Africa - where Sufi orders served as primary vehicles for Islamization.

name: Caucasus
description: The Caucasus - stronghold of the Naqshbandiyya-Khalidiyya.

name: Southeast Asia
description: Maritime Southeast Asia - Malaysia, Indonesia, Brunei.

name: China
description: China and Central Asian Chinese territories.

name: Global Diaspora
description: Sufi communities in the modern West.
